campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority's investment report for March 2026, adopted a new policy about how city property can be used, and discussed efforts to fill open city jobs and improve employee retention.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment report for March 2026
New city policy restricting use of city-owned property for federal civil enforcement
City job openings and recruitment/retention strategies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing to address objections about cleaning up hazardous conditions on properties identified by the County, and then voted to move forward with the cleanup work. The council also adopted a new technology policy for the city and established a process for residents to request accommodations.
keyTopics: Hazardous property abatement - County identified properties requiring cleanup
Public objections hearing - residents had opportunity to voice concerns
New Technology Disruption Policy replacing previous city guidelines
Reasonable accommodations requests process for residents

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to prepare for upcoming council sessions by reviewing and organizing agenda items for May 19 and May 26, 2026. The council also addressed scheduling for the fiscal year 2027 meetings, made committee appointments, and processed a travel request.
keyTopics: Finalized agenda items and timing for May 19, 2026 council meeting
Fiscal Year 2026-2027 council meeting schedule
City Council committee, board, and commission appointments by the Mayor
Travel request approval (Council Member Foley)

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: This notice announced how residents can participate in Mountain View's May 14, 2026 City Council meeting. Residents were invited to submit questions in advance and attend meetings for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Instructions to submit questions before the meeting via online form
Information about board and commission meeting locations and agendas
How to contact the City Manager's Office with agenda questions
Where to view official agendas and staff reports (City Clerk's Office)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on May 5, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting. The closed session prevented the public from attending discussions about certain sensitive matters, while the regular meeting included a formal salute to the flag.
keyTopics: Closed-session discussion
Small business agenda item
Regular council meeting convened after special session

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held an initial planning session to review Cupertino's budget proposal for fiscal year 2026-27. The meeting also covered requirements for lobbyists who try to influence city decisions to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance
Rules for who must disclose lobbying activities to the city

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on May 14, 2026 covered legal disclosure requirements for residents. The council discussed a 90-day time limit for filing lawsuits or legal challenges to city decisions.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline for filing lawsuits against city decisions
Legal challenge procedures under California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6
Quasi-adjudicative decision disclosure requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve major spending plans, including a five-year capital improvement budget for 2027-2031 and a detailed look at next year's operating budget. The council also adopted a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and updated their meeting procedures.
keyTopics: 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan approval
FY 2026-27 Operating Budget presentation
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adoption
Updates to City Council meeting procedures and handbook

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on a proposal to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units on the parking lot at 450 Lytton Avenue. All units would be affordable housing, and the developer is requesting special approvals under state law to make the project work on this small lot.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units proposed for 450 Lytton Avenue
Building would replace 52-space city parking lot (Lot T)
Six-story, 76,400 sq ft multi-family project on 0.43-acre parcel
Developer seeking density bonus law concessions and waivers
Environmental review (CEQA) underway for project approval
